stem: A manufacturing study measures defect rate across 4 factorys, each containing multiple production lines. factory A has 20 observations with mean 51.1 % (within-factory SD = 5.5). factory B has 15 observations with mean 48.3 % (within-factory SD = 5.1). factory C has 15 observations with mean 56.5 % (within-factory SD = 7.1). factory D has 20 observations with mean 58.5 % (within-factory SD = 7.2). The between-factory standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 4.9 and 12.1 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true defect rate for factory D, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-factory variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for factory D: 58.3 % (shrunk from raw 58.5). Range [58.0, 58.4] as between-factory SD varies from 4.9 to 12.1. Pooled mean: 53.8.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 53.8 % (weighted average of 4 factory means).
Shrinkage factor for factory D: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/20). At σ_between=8.1: B = 0.962.
Interval [58.0, 58.4] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (58.5) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (53.8) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
